skill.name: superpowers-lab
skill.description: Guidance for using a Claude superpowers lab environment and related workflow patterns.
skill.summary: Helps users explore Claude superpowers lab workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported two hardcoded URLs and two weak cryptography alerts. Review found only metadata, prose, and repository links in SKILL.md; no executable code, cryptographic use, prompt injection, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs on SKILL.md lines 4 and 22 point to the source repository. They do not execute network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both findings are repository references in metadata and Markdown. No code path or command triggers a network request.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts on SKILL.md lines 3 and 20 are false positives in descriptive prose. No algorithm is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain a description and guidance sentence only. There is no cryptographic implementation or configuration.

content.value_statement: Teams need a clear starting point when testing Claude superpowers lab workflows. This skill provides concise guidance for exploring lab patterns with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Introduces the superpowers-lab purpose and intended use.
Points users to the upstream source repository for more details.
Provides high-level guidance for lab environment work.
Supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code marketplace discovery.
Offers a simple workflow reference without extra scripts.
limitations: Does not include executable tools, scripts, or automation.
Does not define detailed setup commands for the lab environment.
Does not validate results or run tests by itself.
Relies on the linked repository for deeper implementation details.

use_cases.title: Start Lab Exploration
use_cases.description: Use the skill to understand when a Claude superpowers lab workflow may fit a project.

use_cases.title: Review Workflow Fit
use_cases.description: Use the skill as a lightweight checklist before adopting lab-based experimentation.

use_cases.title: Compare Tool Support
use_cases.description: Use the skill to frame Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatibility discussions.

prompt_templates.title: Understand the Lab
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain what this superpowers lab skill is for and when I should use it.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a First Trial
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a small trial using this lab workflow for one project task.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Workflow Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess whether this lab workflow fits my team process, constraints, and supported tools.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Experiment
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an advanced experiment plan for comparing lab workflow outcomes across Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

output_examples.input: Explain when to use this skill.
output_examples.output: Use it when you need a concise orientation for Claude superpowers lab workflows and related experimentation.

output: Define one task for the lab workflow.
Choose the supported AI tool to test.
Record outcomes, gaps, and next steps.

output_examples.output: Main concerns include limited built-in detail, reliance on the linked source, and absence of executable automation.

best_practices: Start with a small task before applying the workflow broadly.
Review the linked source repository before depending on implementation details.
Document observed results so experiments can be compared later.
anti_patterns: Do not treat this skill as an installer or automation package.
Do not assume it validates security, quality, or performance outcomes.
Do not use it as the only source for production process decisions.

faq.question: Does this skill run code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill contains guidance text and links only.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is the repository link a security issue?
faq.answer: No direct issue was found. It is a documentation link, not an executed network request.

faq.question: Does it include setup steps?
faq.answer: No. It provides high-level guidance and points to the source repository.

faq.question: Can it verify lab results?
faq.answer: No. Users must define their own checks and review the outcomes.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. No malicious behavior, prompt injection, or executable risk was found.
